drop_reason: VERIFIER_INERT: tests/install_config.json has FAIL_TO_PASS=[] and PASS_TO_PASS=[], so tests/test.sh falls back to overall pass-rate from `bun x vitest run --reporter=verbose` against /workspace/pi-mono. The session's actual fix (case-insensitive bidirectional tool-name normalization in packages/ai/src/providers/anthropic.ts: replace the `claudeCodeToolNames` record with a `claudeCodeTools` array + `ccToolLookup` Map; remove the wrong `find -> Glob` mapping; remove `ls -> Ls` since CC has no `Ls`/`LS` tool; extend the canonical CC tool list with `AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode, ExitPlanMode, KillShell, NotebookEdit, Skill, Task, TaskOutput, TodoWrite, WebFetch, WebSearch`) has no behavioral coverage in the existing pi-mono workspace test suite at base commit `cddd50e297dc22a3ee55cf842b6cd7ee5dcc9ed0` — `grep -rln 'toClaudeCodeName|fromClaudeCodeName|claudeCodeToolNames' packages/` returns only `packages/ai/src/providers/anthropic.ts` itself, no tests. Repo-wide vitest pass-rate moves on unrelated work (the pi-mono workspace has 74 .test.ts files, 24 of which are env/OAuth-conditional skipIf), giving an unstable noise signal that does not discriminate the buggy state from the fix. Same VERIFIER_INERT pathology as pi-mono-auto-d8c68afc (also `_upgraded_from_legacy: true` with empty FAIL_TO_PASS). Override of prior batch's KEEP rationale ('good task with tests in patch'): the patch's added tests (packages/ai/test/anthropic-tool-name-normalization.test.ts, 205 lines, 4 it() blocks) are wrapped in `describe.skipIf(!oauthToken)` and every test calls `stream(model, context, { apiKey: oauthToken })` against api.anthropic.com — they are OAuth-conditional integration tests that resolve `~/.pi/agent/auth.json` via test/oauth.ts, which does not exist in the sandbox. Per the rubric (`no_network_during_tests`, no live API calls in test.sh), these tests will be SKIPPED, not PASSED, and cannot anchor F2P scoring.
notes: Canonical patch is clean (2 files: M packages/ai/src/providers/anthropic.ts +27/-12, A packages/ai/test/anthropic-tool-name-normalization.test.ts +205/-0; no scratch files to strip; matches the upstream landed code at HEAD where ccToolLookup/claudeCodeTools live). Repair path exists but is out-of-scope for this curator round: tests/test.sh.legacy.bak (preserved in the tests/ directory, 18.5KB) contains 6 well-designed inline-node behavioral F2P gates (t1_f2p_no_semantic_remap 0.25, t1_f2p_case_insensitive_forward 0.20, t4_f2p_test_file_added_and_runs 0.15, t4_f2p_reverse_case_insensitive 0.15, t6_f2p_extended_canonical_tool_list 0.15, t6_f2p_round_trip_identity 0.10) that exercise toClaudeCodeName/fromClaudeCodeName via dist/providers/_anthropic_testable.js with re-export shims for unexported symbols — implementation-agnostic, anti-over-fit, includes round-trip identity. ANTI-OVER-FIT FINDING on the legacy gate set: t6_f2p_extended_canonical_tool_list expects ≥6 of {Agent, CronCreate, CronDelete, CronList, EnterWorktree, ExitWorktree, ScheduleWakeup, TaskStop, ToolSearch}, but the canonical patch's claudeCodeTools array adds {AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode, ExitPlanMode, KillShell, NotebookEdit, Skill, Task, TaskOutput, TodoWrite, WebFetch, WebSearch} — 0/9 overlap. The legacy gate is over-fit to a later/different patch version and would zero on the canonical fix; restoration MUST rewrite this gate to use the patch's actual additions (or accept any ≥6 distinct PascalCase tool names beyond the original 6, which is more uniqueness-tolerant). The other 5 legacy gates align with the canonical patch and are usable as-is. Restoration also requires (a) updating install_config.json with FAIL_TO_PASS gate IDs and a custom test_cmd that invokes the inline-node harness, OR (b) reverting test.sh to its inline-gate legacy shape (which is the simpler path given the legacy.bak is verbatim). v0.4.3 release replayed this task across cohorts (analysis/v043_per_turn_analysis.json line 587, template_alias `pi-mono-auto-db328990__7efe61b3`) under the legacy gate setup, so historical scoring used the behavioral gates — only the post-upgrade install_config-driven test.sh is inert. Recommendation: this task is salvageable on a future repair pass; current verdict is DROP from active suite until either the legacy gates are restored (with the t6 gate rewritten to match the actual patch) or a non-OAuth vitest test is authored that exercises convertTools / convertContentBlocks tool-name conversion via a faux-provider mock.
